description: You are a criminal investigator analyzing a seized USB drive for evidence of anti-forensic timestamp manipulation (timestomping). Your mission: (1) Create a forensic case named 'Timestamp_Anomaly_2024' with case number 'INV-TSA-007' in Autopsy. (2) Add /home/ga/evidence/ntfs_undel.dd as a Disk Image data source; enable 'File Type Identification' and 'Recent Activity' ingest modules, disable others. (3) After ingest, analyze MAC timestamps for all files using Autopsy's UI and TSK command-line tools (e.g., fls, istat) to identify anomalies. (4) Write a MAC timestamp inventory to /home/ga/Reports/mac_timestamps.csv (pipe-delimited) with header: FILENAME|FULL_PATH|ALLOCATED|MODIFIED|ACCESSED|CREATED|CHANGED|SIZE_BYTES. Use 'Y' or 'N' for ALLOCATED. Timestamps should be ISO 8601 (e.g., 2004-03-21T14:30:00). (5) Write an anomaly report to /home/ga/Reports/timestamp_anomalies.txt with exactly these sections: 'VOLUME_ACTIVITY_WINDOW: <earliest_date> to <latest_date>', 'TOTAL_FILES_ANALYZED: <N>', 'TOTAL_ALLOCATED: <N>', 'TOTAL_DELETED: <N>', 'ANOMALY_CREATED_AFTER_MODIFIED: <N>' (count of files where Created > Modified), 'ANOMALY_FUTURE_TIMESTAMPS: <N>' (count of files with dates after 2024-01-15), 'ANOMALY_PRE_2000_TIMESTAMPS: <N>' (count of files with dates before 2000-01-01), 'ANOMALY_SI_FN_DIVERGENCE: <N>' (count of files where $SI and $FN timestamps differ by >24h, or UNABLE_TO_DETERMINE), and 'FINDINGS: <1-5 sentences assessing manipulation>'.
